items.name: ASTROIDS
items.desc: Rotate and thrust your ship, shoot asteroids that split into smaller pieces. Screen wraps. Survive and score. Inspired by the 1979 vector arcade classic.

items.name: BOMBMAN
items.desc: Place bombs on a grid to destroy walls and enemies. Collect power-ups for range, speed, and extra bombs. Bombs chain-react. Clear all enemies to advance.

items.name: BREAK OUT
items.desc: Move a paddle to bounce a ball into rows of bricks. Clear all bricks to win. Ball speeds up; paddle shrinks after hitting the ceiling.

items.name: BURGER TIME
items.desc: Walk across burger ingredients to drop them down platforms. Dodge Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, and Mr. Egg. Assemble all burgers to clear the level.

items.name: CENTIPEED
items.desc: Shoot a centipede weaving down through a mushroom field. Hit a segment and it splits in two. Clear the centipede to advance.

items.name: DEFENDR
items.desc: Side-scrolling shooter. Fly left or right, shoot aliens, rescue humanoids from abduction. Radar shows the full map. Hold both buttons for a smart bomb.

items.name: DIG DIG
items.desc: Dig tunnels underground and inflate enemies with your pump until they pop, or lure them under falling rocks. Clear all enemies to advance.

items.name: FROGGY
items.desc: Cross five lanes of traffic, then hop across logs and turtles to reach home. Guide all five frogs home to clear the level. Don’t get hit or fall in the water.

items.name: GALAXA
items.desc: Fixed-position shooter. Move left/right and fire at alien formations. Survive waves of diving attacks.

items.name: GOLD RUNNER
items.desc: Collect all gold on each level, then escape to the top. Dig holes to trap guards. 150 levels.

items.name: INVADERS
items.desc: Shoot rows of aliens before they reach the bottom. They speed up as you destroy them. Inspired by the 1978 classic.

items.name: LUNAR LANDER
items.desc: Manage thrust and rotation to soft-land on a lunar surface. Land gently on a flat pad to score. Too much speed and you crash.

items.name: MONKEY KONG
items.desc: Climb platforms, jump over barrels, and reach the top. Inspired by the game that started the platformer genre.

items.name: MS. PAK-MAN
items.desc: Eat all dots while avoiding ghosts. Faster than PAK-MAN with multiple mazes and semi-random ghost AI.

items.name: NITE DRIVER
items.desc: Steer between roadside posts against a black background. Stay on the road at increasing speeds. Inspired by one of the earliest first-person driving games.

items.name: PAK-MAN
items.desc: Eat all dots in the maze. Power pellets let you eat ghosts temporarily. Each ghost has unique AI: Blinky chases, Pinky ambushes, Inky flanks, Clyde wanders.

items.name: PING
items.desc: Two paddles, one ball. First to 7 wins. The simplest game that’s still fun.

items.name: Q*BIT
items.desc: Hop on cubes in an isometric pyramid to change their colors. Change every cube to advance. Avoid Coily the snake and other enemies. Don’t fall off the edges.

items.name: SNAKE
items.desc: Eat food, grow longer, don’t hit yourself or the walls. One of the most widely played games ever made.

controls.Space: Start game / Restart after game over

items.name: TETROMINOS
items.desc: Stack falling tetrominoes, clear complete lines. Game ends when pieces reach the top.

items.name: ARCANOID
items.desc: Brick-breaking with power-ups: extended paddle, lasers, slow ball, multi-ball, extra lives. Clear all the bricks to advance through the levels.

items.name: DND
items.desc: Explore rooms, fight monsters, collect treasure. Real-time dungeon crawl: move with the joystick, shoot arrows with the button, and keep an eye on your arrow supply.

items.name: INDIE 500
items.desc: Top-down time-attack racing on an oval track. Complete 7 laps before the 60-second timer runs out - drift off the track and you grind to a halt.

items.name: JAZZBALL
items.desc: Trap bouncing balls by building walls. Claim 75% of the area to advance. Don’t let a ball hit a wall while it’s forming.

controls.Arrow Keys: Move cursor and set wall direction

items.name: PIPE MAZE
items.desc: Place pipe segments before the water arrives. Keep the flow going as long as you can - every pipe the water passes through scores.

items.name: SKI RUN
items.desc: Ski downhill, dodging trees, rocks, and dogs. The Abominable Snowman chases you eventually.

items.name: SPACE CRUISE
items.desc: Side-scrolling starship game. Navigate asteroid fields, manage shields, fire lasers. Survive as long as possible.

items.name: TRASHBLASTER
items.desc: Shoot upward to blast falling garbage before it stacks to the top.

items.name: 3D MONSTR MAZE
items.desc: Escape a T-Rex stalking you through a first-person 3D maze. Based on one of the earliest survival horror games.

controls.Space: Sprint forward 2 cells if both open

items.name: AGAR.IO
items.desc: Absorb smaller cells, avoid bigger ones. L button boosts speed, R button ejects mass. Based on the browser game that launched the .io genre.

items.name: BLOONS
items.desc: Pop waves of balloons by throwing darts. Aim and time your throws to clear each round.

controls.Button (hold): Hold to charge power, release to fire

items.name: BLOONS TD
items.desc: Tower defense. Place and upgrade towers along the path to pop waves of balloons before they reach the end.

controls.Button: Place tower / select tower / upgrade / cycle type

items.name: FLAPPY BIRD
items.desc: Press a button to flap, thread through pipes. One hit and you’re done.

items.name: GEODASH
items.desc: One-button platformer. Hold the button to jump; the level scrolls at a fixed speed. Hit any obstacle and you restart.

controls.Space: Jump (tap or hold for repeated jumps)

items.name: PORTAL
items.desc: Navigate puzzle rooms using portals. L button places a blue portal, R button places an orange one. Walk into one, emerge from the other. Reach the exit to clear each room.

items.name: POWDER GAME
items.desc: Falling-sand particle physics sandbox. Place 13 materials and watch them interact: sand piles, water flows, fire spreads through oil, gunpowder explodes. No score or goal — a creative toy.

items.name: STACK
items.desc: Press the button to drop a sliding block onto the stack. Misalignment shrinks the next platform. Game ends when the block gets too small. Score by stacking as high as possible.

items.name: STICK RUN
items.desc: Endless runner with a stick figure. Press the button to jump over obstacles. Score by distance. Procedurally generated levels.
Slide numbered tiles on a 4×4 grid. Matching tiles merge and double. Reach the 2048 tile to win. Game ends when the board fills with no moves left.

controls.Arrow Keys: Slide all tiles in that direction

items.name: LITE OUT
items.desc: Press a light and its neighbors toggle. Turn all lights off to win. Solvable with linear algebra over GF(2), but trial and error works too.

items.name: MASTER CODE
items.desc: Guess a hidden color code in limited tries. Each guess gets feedback: right color in right place, or right color in wrong place. Deduce the code before your guesses run out.

items.name: RUSH HR
items.desc: Slide cars and trucks on a grid to free the red car through the exit. Vehicles only move along their length. Puzzles range from easy to very hard.

controls.Action: Select vehicle, then direction to slide

items.name: SAIMON
items.desc: Four colored quadrants flash in sequence. Repeat the pattern. Each round adds one step. Game ends when you get the sequence wrong.

items.name: TAP IT
items.desc: Follow commands (bop, twist, pull) using button presses and directional inputs. Commands get faster each round. Miss one and you’re out.

items.name: 15 PUZZLE
items.desc: Slide 15 numbered tiles into order using the single empty space. The “14-15” variant is mathematically impossible.

items.name: BOWLING
items.desc: Ten-pin bowling. Time your release and aim your angle. Standard scoring — strikes, spares, and splits over 10 frames.

controls.Space: Lock position / confirm aim / confirm power / navigate

items.name: DARTS
items.desc: Timing-based darts: press the button to lock the sweeping aim, first across then down. Five rounds, highest total wins - hit double and triple tiles for bonus points.

controls.Joystick: Cancel Y-aim back to X-aim / navigate mode select

items.name: PINBALL
items.desc: Pinball with flippers, bumpers, ramps, and a plunger. L button controls the left flipper, R button the right. Hit targets to score; don’t let the ball drain.

items.name: POOL
items.desc: 8-ball billiards. Aim with the joystick, set power, and shoot. Pocket all your balls (stripes or solids) then sink the 8-ball to win.

items.name: SHUFFLEBOARD
items.desc: Slide pucks down a board to land in the highest-scoring zone. Set angle and power. Knock opponents off if you can.

controls.Space: Lock aim / confirm power / navigate mode select
controls.Joystick: Cancel power back to aim / navigate mode select

items.name: CHECKERS
items.desc: Jump and capture diagonally. Reach the far side to king a piece. Capture all opponent pieces or block all their moves to win. Solved in 2007 — perfect play always draws.

items.name: CHESS
items.desc: 64 squares, 32 pieces. Checkmate the opponent’s king to win. Each piece type rendered as a recognizable sprite on the LED grid.

controls.Button: Select piece / Confirm move (press same piece to deselect)

items.name: CONNECT FOUR
items.desc: Drop colored discs into columns. Connect four in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) to win. Solved in 1988 — first player wins with perfect play.

items.name: GO
items.desc: Place black or white stones to surround territory. The player controlling more territory wins. Simple rules, deep strategy.

controls.Joystick: Move cursor (up past top = PASS zone)

items.name: MANCALA
items.desc: Sow seeds around pits, capture your opponent’s. The player with the most seeds wins. One of the oldest known games.

items.name: REVERSI
items.desc: Place discs to outflank your opponent’s and flip them to your color. The player with the most discs when the board is full wins. Corner control is key.

items.name: DRIFT
items.desc: Terrain-sculpting sandbox. One button cycles the tool - raise, lower, smooth, spring, water - and the other applies it at the cursor. Watch the water find its level.

controls.Space: Cycle tool: Raise / Lower / Smooth / Spring / Water

items.name: FISHING
items.desc: Drop your line and reel in as many fish as you can before the 90-second timer runs out.

controls.Space/Z: Hook fish (hold or mash to reel)

items.name: LASER MIRRORS
items.desc: Rotate and place mirrors to redirect the laser beam to its target. A puzzle of angles and reflection.

items.name: WINDOW WASHER
items.desc: Clean every window on the building before time runs out. Ride the scaffold and scrub.

items.name: ALL GAMES
items.desc: Every single-player game in the cabinet, shuffled into one endless pick-and-play stream.

items.name: ARCADE MIX
items.desc: A shuffled run through the classic arcade cabinet games — the quarter-eaters of the golden age.

items.name: CLASSICS
items.desc: A shuffle of the all-time greats, drawn from across the eras.

items.name: PUZZLE
items.desc: A shuffle of the brain-teasers and puzzle games — slower, thinkier play.

items.name: QUICK PLAY
items.desc: Fast, pick-up-and-play games for a spare minute — short loops, instant restarts.

items.name: SHOOTERS
items.desc: A mix of the space shooters and blasters — everything with a fire button.

items.name: BOIDS
items.desc: Three rules per agent: stay close, match heading, don’t collide. Flocking behavior emerges with no central control. The same algorithm is used in film VFX, robotics, and crowd simulation.

items.name: BOIDS LAB
items.desc: The Boids flocking model turned into an explorable parameter space. The joystick sweeps separation against cohesion; watch tight schools loosen into scattered wandering and back.

items.name: CYCLIC
items.desc: Each state can only be consumed by the next state in the cycle. Produces rotating spirals and expanding amoeba-like structures. Also known as Demon Spirals.

items.name: CYCLIC LAB
items.desc: Explore the cyclic cellular automaton by hand. The joystick adjusts the number of states and the neighbor threshold, tuning between still noise and sweeping demon spirals.

items.name: FADERS
items.desc: Cells glow, fade, and reactivate based on their neighbors' brightness. Produces cascading pulses and traveling waves of light.

items.name: GRAY-SCOTT
items.desc: Turing proposed that reaction-diffusion could explain biological pattern formation. The Gray-Scott model uses two parameters: F (feed rate, how fast chemical U is replenished) and K (kill rate, how fast chemical V is removed). Different F/K combinations produce distinct pattern families — spots, stripes, coral, and worms. The joystick explores the Pearson parameter space; overlay labels show the Greek-letter classification (Alpha, Beta, etc.) from Pearson’s 1993 taxonomy.

items.name: GRAY-SCOTT LAB
items.desc: Reaction-diffusion patterns computed on a grid. Four pattern families: spots, stripes, coral, and worms. The mechanism Turing proposed for how leopards get their spots.

controls.Left/Right: Adjust F parameter within family range

items.name: GYRE
items.desc: Rotating vortex patterns driven by simple agent rules. Particles orbit local centers of rotation, forming spirals that merge and split.

items.name: HODGE
items.desc: Models the Belousov-Zhabotinsky chemical reaction, a real oscillator that produces spiral waves in a petri dish. Self-organizing spirals emerge from random initial conditions.

items.name: HODGE LAB
items.desc: A hands-on version of the Hodgepodge Machine. Adjust the growth rate and maximum state count to steer the Belousov-Zhabotinsky spiral waves between calm and chaotic.

items.name: JELLYFISH
items.desc: Particle Life variant tuned for drifting, pulsing jellyfish motion. Multiple species with asymmetric attraction and repulsion forces produce bell shapes trailing tentacles.

items.name: LENIA
items.desc: Continuous cellular automaton that produces lifelike self-organizing patterns. Smooth kernel functions replace discrete neighbor counts. Creatures emerge, move, interact, and die — artificial life from pure math.

items.name: LENIA LAB
items.desc: Steer Lenia's continuous life forms directly. The joystick moves the growth center and width, morphing the smooth kernel so creatures grow, dissolve, or hold their shape.

items.name: LIFE
items.desc: Four rules about birth, survival, and death produce gliders, oscillators, and self-replicating structures. Proven Turing-complete. The most famous cellular automaton.

items.name: MITOSIS
items.desc: A simulation of cell division. Cells grow, duplicate their contents, pinch in half, and separate.

items.name: MITOSIS LAB
items.desc: The cell-division simulation opened up for tinkering. Trade growth rate against competition strength and watch colonies bloom, crowd, and pinch apart.

items.name: QUARKS
items.desc: Particles with charge-like properties that attract and repel, forming fleeting bonds and structures. Simple local rules produce behavior reminiscent of particle physics.

items.name: QUARKS LAB
items.desc: Explore the Quarks automaton's parameter space. The joystick sets particle count and interaction radius, tuning how charge-like cells bond and break.

items.name: RUCKER PAINT
items.desc: CelLab flowing paint cellular automaton by Rudy Rucker and John Walker. Globby streams of color flow and merge on a ring topology.

items.name: RUG
items.desc: An averaging cellular automaton. Each cell becomes the average of its neighbors, then saturates, producing symmetric patterns that look like woven rugs.

items.name: RUG LAB
items.desc: The averaging 'rug' automaton, made adjustable. Sweep the increment and state count to weave different symmetric, textile-like patterns.

controls.Left/Right: Adjust increment (the +N in the rug rule)

items.name: SANDPILE
items.desc: The Abelian Sandpile Model. Drop grains one at a time; chain-reaction avalanches form fractal patterns. Demonstrates self-organized criticality — the mechanism behind power laws in earthquakes and markets.

items.name: SLIME
items.desc: Agents deposit and follow chemical trails, mimicking slime mold (Physarum polycephalum). Simple deposit-sense-turn rules produce networks similar to real transport systems.

items.name: SLIME LAB
items.desc: Two slime-mold colonies compete under your control. Adjust growth chance against attack power to decide whether one colony overruns the other or they reach a truce.

items.name: STARWARS
items.desc: A totalistic cellular automaton with birth/survival rules. Produces long-lived oscillating structures and traveling sparks.

items.name: WOLFRAM
items.desc: 256 elementary 1D cellular automata, each defined by a single number. Rule 30 generates randomness; Rule 110 is Turing-complete.

controls.Space: Change rule (new pattern rolls in from bottom)

items.name: BAKING
items.desc: Baking ratios and formulas displayed as baker’s percentages with proportional ingredient bars. 14 recipes across 4 families: bread, pizza, pastry, and quick breads.

items.name: BUTCHER
items.desc: Primal-cut charts for beef, pork, lamb, and chicken. Each animal is a pixel-art silhouette with its butcher's regions shaded in; drill into any primal to see the retail cuts and how to cook them.

items.name: FLAVORS
items.desc: Regional cuisine flavor profiles showing key ingredients and five intensity bars (sweet, sour, salty, spicy, umami). 13 cuisines across 6 regions from East Asia to the Middle East.

items.name: KNIFE CUTS
items.desc: A knife-skills flipbook. Each cut animates on the cutting board and holds on the finished result, with dimensions and best uses. Nine cuts across three families: strips, dice, and special.

items.name: PANTRY
items.desc: Cooking building blocks — aromatics, roux, stocks, and herb bundles with ratio blocks showing component proportions. 14 items across 4 families with method and use descriptions.

items.name: PASTA
items.desc: Pixel art pasta shape reference. 20 shapes, each rendered as a small illustration with name, Italian meaning, region of origin, and sauce pairings. 5 families: long, tube, shaped, filled, and sheet.

items.name: SAUCES
items.desc: Mother sauce reference guide. 38 sauces organized into 7 families, each showing its parent sauce and the ingredient you add to create it. From béchamel to hollandaise and beyond.

items.name: SPICES
items.desc: The recipes behind the world's great spice blends. Each shows its origin, meaning, component spices, and the dishes it belongs in — the micro view of what's actually inside ras el hanout, garam masala, and more.

items.name: COINS
items.desc: Historical coins rendered from Wikimedia Commons and the Met Museum Open Access collection. Ancient Greek, Roman, medieval, and early modern currency.

items.name: FLAGS
items.desc: National flags of the world rendered on the LED matrix. 200+ flags loaded from PNG bitmaps. Joystick to browse; overlay shows country name. Data from flagcdn.com.

items.name: KNITTING
items.desc: Stranded-knitting stitch charts rendered as pixel grids. Each two-color pattern tiles across the panel and animates row by row, a knitting needle tracking the active line.

items.name: LANGUAGE
items.desc: World languages and their relationships. Script samples, language family trees, and translation comparisons.

items.name: POTTERY
items.desc: World ceramic traditions photographed from the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Open Access collection. Real vessels shown at 64×64 with their culture, date, and technique.

items.name: SCRIPTS
items.desc: Writing systems from around the world — Latin, Arabic, Devanagari, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and more. Rendered using fontTools. Each script shown with sample glyphs and stroke animations.

items.name: TESTAMENT
items.desc: Biblical narratives visualized as animated LED scenes. Key moments from Genesis through Revelation rendered in pixel art.

items.name: WEAVING
items.desc: How cloth is actually made. A top-down view of warp and weft threads interlacing, weft rows building the weave from the top down. 46 structures spanning 7,000 years and every inhabited continent, in 18 traditional palettes.

items.name: AGAR.IO
items.desc: AI-controlled Agar.io for idle screen demos. The AI chases nearest edible target and flees threats.

items.name: ALL
items.desc: Auto-cycling visual playlists that rotate through curated sets of visuals. Hold 2 seconds to exit back to menu (handled by main loop).

items.name: ARCANOID
items.desc: Arkanoid plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI predicts where the ball will land and moves the paddle to intercept. AI Strategy: - Launch ball automatically when waiting - Predict ball trajectory accounting for wall bounces - Move paddle toward predicted landing position - Prioritize catching falling powerups - Fire laser automatically when powerup is active

items.name: ASTROIDS
items.desc: Asteroids plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates, shoots asteroids, and dodges threats. AI Strategy: - Find nearest asteroid (or UFO if present) - Rotate ship toward target - Thrust to approach (but not too close) - Shoot when aimed at target - Dodge when threats are very close

items.name: BLOONS
items.desc: Bloons plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI simulates trajectories and picks the angle/power that pops the most bloons per dart (including child chains). AI Strategy: - Sweep angles × power levels, simulate full dart flight - Score each trajectory by total pops (dart passes through bloons) - Child bloons from multi-layer pops are included in the count - Refine around best with fine grid search

items.name: BLOONS TD
items.desc: Bloons Tower Defense plays itself. The AI places towers near path bends, upgrades them, and lets waves auto-start for income. Skips steps it can't afford and retries when money comes in. AI Strategy: - Walk through a build order of placements and upgrades - Skip steps that are too expensive; retry when income arrives - Directly set tower type (demo privilege) to avoid cycling bugs - Idle gracefully between builds, letting waves generate income

items.name: BOMBMAN
items.desc: Bomberman plays itself using pathfinding AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates around bombs, destroys bricks, kills enemies, and finds the exit. AI Strategy: - Calculate danger zones from active bombs (bomb positions + fire_power range in 4 directions) - If in danger zone, pathfind to nearest safe tile - If safe and near a brick wall, place bomb then move away - If exit is revealed and all enemies dead, pathfind to exit - Target bricks near enemies to kill them - Collect powerups when safe

items.name: BOWLING
items.desc: Bowling plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI positions the ball, aims for the pocket, and times the power/spin. AI Strategy: - Select 1P mode automatically - Position ball slightly off-center (pocket approach) - Aim toward the 1-3 pocket for strikes - Time power meter for center hit (max power, minimal spin) - Add slight variation for realistic play

items.name: BREAK OUT
items.desc: Breakout plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI predicts where the ball will land and moves the paddle to intercept. AI Strategy: - Launch ball automatically when waiting - Predict ball trajectory accounting for wall bounces - Move paddle toward predicted landing position

items.name: BURGER TIME
items.desc: Peter Pepper plays himself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates platforms and ladders to drop burger ingredients. AI Strategy: - Find burger ingredients that haven't been dropped yet - Navigate to the platform containing the target ingredient - Use ladders to reach higher/lower floors - Walk across ingredients to drop them down - Avoid enemies (hotdog, egg, pickle) - keep distance - Use pepper to stun enemies when they get too close

items.name: CENTIPEED
items.desc: Centipede plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI shoots at centipede segments while avoiding the spider. AI Strategy: - Prioritize spider if close (within 15px) - it's dangerous and worth 600 points - Target nearest centipede head segment - If no heads, target nearest body segment - Move toward target's x position - Move up/down to get clear shot (avoid mushrooms blocking) - Shoot when roughly aligned

items.name: CHECKERS
items.desc: Two AIs play checkers against each other for idle screen demos. AI emulates Arthur Samuel's Checkers Program (1959) - the pioneering machine learning program developed at IBM that beat a Connecticut state champion in 1962. Historical AI Strategy: - Alpha-beta search with 4-6 ply depth - Evaluation features: piece count, kings, advancement, center control - "Piece advantage" and "denial of occupancy" heuristics - Original learned feature weights through self-play Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arthur_Samuel#Checkers_program

items.name: CHESS
items.desc: Two chess AIs play against each other for idle screen demos. AI emulates the Bernstein Chess Program (1957) - one of the first complete chess programs, developed by Alex Bernstein at IBM. Historical AI Strategy: - 4-ply minimax search (2 moves ahead per side) - Only considers 7 "most plausible" moves per position - Evaluation: material, mobility, area control, king defense - Original ran on IBM 704, took ~8 minutes per move Reference: https://www.chessprogramming.org/The_Bernstein_Chess_Program

items.name: CONNECT FOUR
items.desc: Two AIs play Connect 4 using bitboard-based alpha-beta search. Historical context: Connect 4 was solved by Victor Allis in 1988 and independently by James D. Allen. First player wins with perfect play starting from the center column. This engine uses techniques from that era: bitboard position encoding for O(1) win detection (John Tromp's shift-AND technique), negamax with alpha-beta pruning, a transposition table, and center-biased move ordering. AI Strategy: - Bitboard representation — each side is a 49-bit integer - O(1) win detection via shift-AND on four directions - Negamax with alpha-beta pruning, iterative deepening to depth 20+ - Transposition table with exact / lower / upper bound entries - Move ordering: center columns first [3, 2, 4, 1, 5, 0, 6] - Evaluation: all 69 four-in-a-row windows scored by threat level, plus center-column positional weighting

items.name: DARTS
items.desc: Darts plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI aims for high-value targets like triple 20 and bullseye. AI Strategy: - Automatically select 1P mode - Track sweeping aim bars - Target triple 20 (top of board, x~32, y~16) or bullseye (x~32, y~32) - Also attempt bonus tiles when convenient - Time throws when sweep line crosses target position

items.name: DEFENDR
items.desc: Defender plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. AI Strategy: - Priority 1: Rescue captured/falling humans (fly to intercept) - Priority 2: Dodge nearby enemy bullets - Priority 3: Attack nearest diving lander that has a human - Priority 4: Attack nearest visible enemy - Auto-fire when facing enemy in range

items.name: DIG DIG
items.desc: Dig Dug plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI digs toward enemies and pumps them until they pop. AI Strategy: - Find nearest enemy that isn't in ghost mode - Move toward that enemy through tunnels or by digging - When adjacent to enemy (within pump range ~8 pixels), hold pump button - Avoid enemies in ghost mode (they can move through dirt) - Avoid falling rocks

items.name: FLAPPY
items.desc: Flappy plays itself by timing flaps to navigate through pipe gaps. AI Strategy: - Look at the next pipe gap - Flap when bird is below the gap center and falling - Account for gravity and upward velocity from flaps

items.name: FROGGY
items.desc: Frogger plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates through traffic and across water platforms. AI Strategy: - Check safety of forward moves (avoid cars, land on platforms) - Look ahead to predict object positions - Prefer moving up when safe, dodge sideways when needed - Target unfilled home slots

items.name: GALAXA
items.desc: Galaga plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI dodges bullets, shoots enemies, and prioritizes UFOs. AI Strategy: - Dodge enemy bullets when they're close and threatening - Prioritize UFO if present - Target the lowest alive enemy in formation or nearest diver - Move toward target x, shoot when aligned

items.name: GEOMETRY
items.desc: Geometry Dash plays itself by timing jumps to clear obstacles. AI Strategy: - Look ahead at upcoming obstacles (spikes, blocks) - Jump when an obstacle is approaching at the right distance - Time jumps to clear spikes and gaps - The game auto-scrolls, so AI just needs to decide when to jump

items.name: GOLD RUNNER
items.desc: Lode Runner plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI collects all gold and escapes via the exit ladder. AI Strategy: - Find nearest gold piece to collect - Pathfind using platforms, ladders, and ropes - Avoid enemies (monks) - find safe routes - Dig holes to trap enemies blocking the path - Once all gold collected, head for the exit ladder at top

items.name: INDIE 500
items.desc: AI races around the oval track for idle screen demos. AI Strategy: - Steer toward a lookahead point on the track centerline - Lookahead distance increases when car drifts off-center (self-correcting) - Always accelerate (hold gas pedal)

items.name: INVADERS
items.desc: Space Invaders plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI tracks aliens, dodges bullets, and prioritizes the UFO. AI Strategy: - Dodge incoming bullets that are dangerously close - Prioritize shooting the UFO when it appears - Target the lowest (most dangerous) alien - Move toward target and shoot when aligned

items.name: JAZZBALL
items.desc: JezzBall plays itself using strategic AI for idle screen demos. The AI builds walls to divide the playing field and trap atoms. AI Strategy: - Track ball positions and predict trajectories - Find safe locations to build walls (away from balls) - Build walls to divide the playing field - Choose horizontal or vertical walls based on ball positions - Aim to trap balls in smaller sections - Wait for balls to clear an area before building

items.name: LITE OUT
items.desc: Lights Out plays itself using the "chase the lights" algorithm. AI Strategy (Chase the Lights): - Process each row from top to bottom - For each lit cell in a row, toggle the cell directly below it - This "pushes" the lit cells downward - After processing all rows, use a lookup table for the bottom row - The bottom row pattern determines which top row cells to toggle - This algorithm is guaranteed to solve any solvable puzzle

items.name: LUNAR LANDER
items.desc: Lunar Lander plays itself using AI to land safely on the moon. AI Strategy: - Control descent velocity - don't fall too fast - Aim for the landing pad horizontally - Use rotation to orient thrust direction - Use main thrust to slow descent when getting close - Keep vertical velocity low and angle upright for safe landing

items.name: MANCALA
items.desc: Two AIs play Mancala using minimax with alpha-beta pruning at depth 8. Handles free turns, captures, and store difference evaluation.

items.name: MONKEY KONG
items.desc: Mario plays himself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates toward Pauline while avoiding barrels. AI Strategy: - Goal: reach Pauline at top - Find nearest safe ladder going up (check for barrels) - If barrel approaching, jump over it - Move toward ladders, climb when reached - On broken ladders, can only climb halfway - Simple pathfinding: move right/left toward ladder x, then climb

items.name: MS. PAK-MAN
items.desc: Ms. Pac-Man plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates toward pellets while avoiding ghosts. AI Strategy: - Find nearest reachable pellet using BFS - Avoid tiles near ghosts (unless powered up) - Chase frightened ghosts for bonus points - Grab power pellets when ghosts are close

items.name: NITE DRIVER
items.desc: Night Driver plays itself with AI navigating the road at night. AI Strategy: - Stay on the road by tracking road edges/markers - Steer toward center of road - Adjust steering based on upcoming curves - Maintain appropriate speed for turns - Counter the curve push force to stay centered

items.name: PAK-MAN
items.desc: Pac-Man plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates toward pellets while avoiding ghosts. AI Strategy: - Find nearest reachable pellet using BFS - Avoid tiles near ghosts (unless powered up) - Chase frightened ghosts for bonus points - Grab power pellets when ghosts are close

items.name: PINBALL
items.desc: Pinball plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI tracks ball position and velocity, predicting trajectory to time flipper hits. AI Strategy: - Launch ball automatically when waiting (charged plunger) - Track ball position and velocity - Activate left flipper when ball approaches from the left side - Activate right flipper when ball approaches from the right side - Predict ball trajectory to time flipper hits

items.name: PING
items.desc: AI vs AI Pong match for idle screen demos. Both paddles are controlled by AI with slight imperfections for natural gameplay. AI Strategy: - Both paddles track the ball's Y position with slight delay - Small random error prevents perfect play - Auto-serves when waiting

items.name: PIPE MAZE
items.desc: Pipe Dream plays itself using strategic AI for idle screen demos. The AI places pipes to keep the flow going as long as possible. AI Strategy: - Build a connected path AHEAD of the flow during countdown - Always ensure the immediate next cell has a matching pipe - Create serpentine paths to maximize pipe count - Discard non-fitting pipes in safe locations away from the path

items.name: POOL
items.desc: AI plays pool for idle screen demos using ghost ball targeting. AI Strategy: - Select 1P mode automatically - AIMING: For each ball × pocket, compute ghost ball position (behind ball, opposite pocket). Check line-of-sight from cue. Score by clear path, distance, cut angle. Aim at best ghost ball. - POWER: Proportional to distance from cue to ghost ball. - SHOOTING/SCORING/FOUL/TURN_CHANGE: no input, let timers handle. - GAME_OVER: restart after 3 second pause.

items.name: Q*BIT
items.desc: Q*bert plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI hops around the pyramid coloring cubes while avoiding enemies. AI Strategy: - Avoid Coily (purple snake) - move away if on same row or adjacent - Catch Slick/Sam if close (worth 300 points) - Find nearest uncolored cube using BFS on pyramid graph - Move in direction that gets closer to target

items.name: REVERSI
items.desc: Two AIs play Othello against each other for idle screen demos. AI emulates Iago/Logistello style programs from the 1980s - the era when Othello programs first achieved master-level play. Historical AI Strategy: - Corner control is paramount (corners can never be flipped) - Edge stability - pieces on edges are hard to flip - Mobility - having more moves available is good - Disc count only matters in endgame - Avoid "X-squares" (diagonal to corners) and "C-squares" (adjacent to corners) Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_Othello

items.name: SHUFFLEBOARD
items.desc: Shuffleboard plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI uses physics-correct power calculations to land pucks in high-scoring zones consistently. AI Strategy: - Select 1P mode automatically - Target zone 4 (70%) or zone 3 (30%) for best scoring - Compute physics-correct power: v0 = sqrt(2 * friction * distance) - Aim near center with minimal variance - Small power variance for natural feel

items.name: SKI RUN
items.desc: SkiFree plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI navigates downhill, avoiding trees, rocks, and other obstacles. AI Strategy: - Look ahead at upcoming obstacles (trees, rocks) - Steer left/right to avoid obstacles within a distance threshold - Prefer center of screen when no obstacles ahead - Use boost occasionally to escape the Yeti

items.name: SNAKE
items.desc: Snake plays itself using BFS pathfinding for idle screen demos. The AI navigates toward food while avoiding self-collision and walls. AI Strategy: - Use BFS from head to food - Avoid tiles occupied by snake body (except tail which will move) - If no path to food, follow tail to stay alive

items.name: SPACE CRUISE
items.desc: SpaceCruise plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI builds math combos by targeting numbers and operators. AI Strategy: - Look for targets that will maximize score - If no combo active, target a number to start one - If have a number, look for an operator - If have number + operator, look for another number to complete combo - Move rocket and crosshair to align with target, then fire

items.name: STACK
items.desc: Stack plays itself using timing prediction for idle screen demos. AI Strategy: - Track the moving block position relative to the target zone - Calculate when the block aligns with the stack below - Press action when alignment is good (within tolerance) - Tolerance decreases as blocks get smaller for precision

items.name: STICK RUNNER
items.desc: Stick Runner plays itself by timing jumps to cross rooftop gaps. AI Strategy: - Look ahead for gaps between buildings - Calculate when to jump based on gap distance and scroll speed - Account for building height differences (jump earlier for higher targets) - Use the game's physics to estimate jump distance

items.name: TETROMINOS
items.desc: Tetris plays itself using AI for idle screen demos. The AI evaluates all possible placements and chooses the best one based on minimizing holes, maximizing line clears, and keeping the stack low. AI Strategy: - For each piece, try all rotations and positions - Score each placement based on: holes, lines cleared, height, bumpiness - Hard drop when in position for fast play

items.name: TRASH BLASTER
items.desc: TrashBlaster plays itself using simple AI for idle screen demos. The AI moves the crosshair to track and blast floating trash. AI Strategy: - Find the nearest trash target - Move crosshair toward target - Fire when aligned with target center - Prioritize larger trash (easier to hit, clears screen faster)

2048 plays itself using a corner strategy for idle screen demos. AI Strategy (Corner Strategy): - Keep the highest tile in the bottom-left corner - Priority order: Down, Left, Right, Up - Only use Up as a last resort - If a move doesn't change the board, try the next in priority - This simple strategy frequently reaches 2048

items.name: 3D MONSTR MAZE
items.desc: AI navigates the maze using BFS to find the exit while fleeing the Rex. Auto-restarts on game over.

items.name: AMIGA BARS
items.desc: Metallic gradient bars bouncing on sine waves. Uses the Amiga copper chip technique — changing colors mid-scanline. A signature effect of late-80s demos.

items.name: CRYSTALLIZE
items.desc: Competing crystal domains grow from random seeds using Monte Carlo dynamics. Grain boundaries form where domains meet. Based on the Potts model — a generalization of the Ising model to multiple spin states. Inspired by an introspection experiment where 94 AI agents independently reported the same phenomenon: ideas crystallizing into competing domains with grain boundaries at their edges. The simulation’s temperature curve is derived from token-usage math measuring how Claude’s output changes under sustained prompting.

items.name: FIRE
items.desc: Classic fire simulation with a heat source at the bottom, upward propagation, and cooling. The algorithm dates back to the demoscene of the early 1990s.

items.name: FLUX
items.desc: Particle streams guided by time-varying vector fields. Particles trace smooth curves, creating ribbons of color.

items.name: FROZEN LAKE
items.desc: A frozen black lake under a dark sky. Derived from convergent imagery that emerged independently across multiple AI imagination experiments.

controls.Action (Space/Z): Presence arrives (hold or tap to sustain)

items.name: LARSON SCAN
items.desc: A bright dot sweeping back and forth with a fading trail — the Cylon eye from Battlestar Galactica (and Kit from Knight Rider). Named after producer Glen A. Larson.

controls.Space: Toggle single bar / full screen mode

items.name: MATRIX
items.desc: Falling green characters with trail fading, from the film’s “digital rain” effect. Designed by Simon Whiteley using characters scanned from a Japanese cookbook.

items.name: MAZE
items.desc: First-person maze navigation in the style of a classic screensaver. The camera auto-navigates through corridors and intersections.

controls.Joystick: Take manual control (auto-walk resumes after idle)

items.name: MOIRE
items.desc: Two overlapping grids, slightly offset and rotating, produce shimmering moiré interference patterns.

items.name: PLASMA
items.desc: Four overlapping sine functions create smoothly animated color fields. One of the quintessential demoscene effects.

items.name: RAINBOW
items.desc: Cycling HSV color gradients sweeping across the panel.

items.name: ROTOZOOM
items.desc: Simultaneous rotation and zoom of a procedural texture using rotation matrix math. A staple of Amiga and Atari ST demos.

items.name: SINESCROLL
items.desc: Text scrolling along a sine wave — the classic demoscene intro effect. Each character bobs independently on a sinusoidal path.

items.name: STARFIELD
items.desc: 3D stars rushing toward you with perspective projection. Dots start small in the center and streak outward as they approach.

items.name: TRANCE
items.desc: Pulsing concentric shapes and strobing color patterns forming an expanding/contracting tunnel effect.

items.name: TRUCHET
items.desc: Quarter-circle tiles placed randomly create flowing organic curves. Discovered by a Dominican priest over 300 years ago.

items.name: TWISTER
items.desc: Vertical bars twist as if wrapped around a spinning cylinder. A demoscene staple creating depth from 2D math.

items.name: XOR
items.desc: XOR the x and y coordinates of each pixel, map the result to color. A single bitwise operation produces Sierpinski-like recursive patterns.

items.name: GRAND MUSEUM
items.desc: A walkable 3D museum in the style of early raycast games. Six themed galleries hang paintings, sprites, and animated rooms on their walls. Use the joystick to wander; the button reads the title plate.

items.name: SALON
items.desc: A walkable 3D gallery housing 116 classical paintings — Van Gogh, Monet, Hokusai, Vermeer, Klimt, and more. Four connected rooms with paintings stacked floor to ceiling. Joystick to walk; button for title plate.

items.name: AQUARIUM
items.desc: A tropical fish tank. Eight species school through swaying plants with rising bubbles. Three color schemes: Tropical, Moonlight, and Sunset.

items.name: BALLOONS
items.desc: Colorful balloons rising and drifting. Pop them or watch them float away.

items.name: CAT
items.desc: A pixel cat walking, sitting, sleeping, and chasing a laser dot.

items.name: CLOCK
items.desc: A working digital clock. Hours, minutes, and seconds on the LED matrix.

controls.Space: Toggle between digital and analog mode

items.name: DVD
items.desc: The bouncing DVD logo screensaver. Changes color on every wall hit. The screen flashes when it hits the corner.

items.name: KNOTS
items.desc: Animated knot tying. Step-by-step demonstrations of bowline, cleat hitch, figure-eight, and more — rendered as colored rope on the LED grid.

items.name: LAVALAMP
items.desc: Wax blobs rise and fall through heated liquid. Metaballs simulate the organic splitting and merging.

items.name: POLAROID
items.desc: An instant camera that “photographs” other visuals in the collection. The image develops slowly in the classic Polaroid white frame.

items.name: SOLITAIRE
items.desc: The classic solitaire victory cascade. Cards tumble and bounce across the screen in arcing trails.

items.name: YOGA
items.desc: Yoga pose sequences. Animated figures flowing through asanas — sun salutations, warrior poses, and balancing sequences.

items.name: ATTRACTORS
items.desc: The Lorenz “butterfly” attractor, the Rössler attractor, and the Thomas attractor. Trajectories that fold through space without ever crossing themselves.

items.name: DBL PENDULUM
items.desc: Two arms, two masses, deterministic equations — yet the trajectory never repeats. The simplest physical system that produces chaos.

items.name: ENTROPY
items.desc: Shannon entropy visualized. Measures the information content and disorder of a system — the foundation of information theory.

items.name: FIBONACCI
items.desc: The Fibonacci sequence visualized. Golden spirals, phyllotaxis patterns, and the golden ratio — the mathematics that appears everywhere in nature.

items.name: FRACTALS
items.desc: Classic fractals rendered in real time: Mandelbrot set, Julia sets, Sierpinski triangle, Koch snowflake, and fractal trees. Joystick to pan and zoom into infinite self-similar detail.

items.name: GAME THEORY
items.desc: Iterated Prisoner’s Dilemma tournament on a spatial grid. Strategies — cooperate, defect, tit-for-tat — compete and evolve. Cooperation can emerge from selfishness.

items.name: MOBIUS
items.desc: A rotating Möbius strip — the one-sided surface discovered in 1858. 3D wireframe or shaded projection spinning in space.

items.name: NETWORK
items.desc: Graph theory and network dynamics. Nodes connect, signals propagate, and emergent structure forms from simple connection rules.

controls.Action: Send signal pulse from random node

items.name: PERCOLATION
items.desc: A phase transition in connectivity. Below a critical threshold, clusters stay isolated. Above it, a spanning cluster suddenly connects the entire grid.

items.name: PI
items.desc: Visualizations of π — its digits mapped to colors, its geometric definition as circumference over diameter, and Monte Carlo estimation by random points in a circle.

items.name: PRIMES
items.desc: Prime number sieves and distributions. The Sieve of Eratosthenes animates as composites are eliminated, revealing the primes that remain.

items.name: SOLIDS
items.desc: The five Platonic solids — tetrahedron, cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, icosahedron — rotating in 3D wireframe. The only regular convex polyhedra that exist.

items.name: SORTING
items.desc: Sorting algorithms visualized. Bubble sort, quicksort, merge sort, and more — each algorithm’s strategy made visible as bars rearrange themselves.

items.name: SPIROGRAPH
items.desc: Hypotrochoid and epitrochoid curves from rolling circles. The plastic drawing toy that taught a generation about roulette curves.

items.name: TESSELLATIONS
items.desc: Tile patterns that cover the plane without gaps or overlaps. Regular, semi-regular, and Penrose tilings — from Islamic geometry to aperiodic order.

items.name: ANTIKYTHERA
items.desc: Ancient Greek analog computer with interlocking bronze gears that predicted astronomical positions and eclipses. Nothing comparable was built for 1,500 years.

items.name: ARCHIMEDES
items.desc: A helical surface inside a cylinder, turned by hand to raise water. Still used today for irrigation and wastewater.

items.name: ASTROLABE
items.desc: A brass instrument for telling time, finding latitude, predicting sunrise/sunset, and locating stars. Used by astronomers and navigators for over a thousand years.

items.name: BEAM ENGINE
items.desc: Watt’s separate condenser transformed Newcomen’s earlier engine into the workhorse of the Industrial Revolution. The rocking beam links piston to flywheel.

items.name: CAMSHAFT
items.desc: A rotating camshaft with eccentric lobes operating valve followers. Converts rotation into precisely timed reciprocating motion — the basis of internal combustion valve timing.

items.name: CURTA
items.desc: Curt Herzstark's handheld mechanical calculator, designed while imprisoned in Buchenwald. Cylindrical Leibniz wheels inside a pepper-grinder housing.

items.name: GRANDFATHER
items.desc: Longcase pendulum clock with escapement mechanism. The pendulum swings, the escape wheel ticks, the hands turn.

items.name: GUTENBERG
items.desc: Movable type printing press. The full mechanical cycle: inking, bed slide, press down, impression, release.

items.name: GYROSCOPE
items.desc: A spinning rotor that resists changes to its orientation. Demonstrates precession, nutation, and conservation of angular momentum.

items.name: JACQUARD
items.desc: Punch cards controlling a weaving loom — the direct ancestor of computer programming. Babbage modeled his Analytical Engine on its logic; Lovelace saw the deeper computational implications.

items.name: LOCOMOTIVE
items.desc: Side-view cutaway with driving wheels, coupling rods, and crankshaft. The crank-slider mechanism converts the piston's linear motion into rotary motion.

items.name: LOOM
items.desc: A working weaving loom. The shuttle passes through the warp threads, the heddles lift, the beater presses. Cloth forms row by row.

items.name: NEWTON
items.desc: Five suspended steel balls transferring momentum. Pull one back, release, and watch the energy pass through.

controls.Button: Cycle number of balls in swing

items.name: ORRERY
items.desc: A clockwork model of the solar system. Brass arms carry planets at their correct relative orbital speeds, with gears visible at the base. Named after the 4th Earl of Orrery.

items.name: PROJECTOR
items.desc: A film projector with spinning reels, film threading through the gate, and a beam of light. 24 still images per second creating the illusion of motion.

items.name: SINGER
items.desc: A sewing machine with needle, bobbin, and feed dog. The needle plunges, the thread loops, the fabric advances.

items.name: TURING MACHINE
items.desc: A working Turing machine running real programs on a tape. The read/write head shuttles back and forth, writing symbols and switching states — the abstract foundation of all computation, made mechanical.

items.name: TYPEWRITER
items.desc: A mechanical typewriter with type bars striking an inked ribbon against paper. The carriage advances, the bell rings at the margin, the return lever sweeps back.

items.name: WATCH
items.desc: A five-gear train: barrel → center → third → fourth → escapement. Balance wheel with hairspring, proper gear meshing, and clock hands. Mechanical timekeeping in miniature.

items.name: 4-STROKE
items.desc: Animated cross-section showing the engine, transmission, and drive train of the car that introduced mass production to automobiles.

items.name: CHORD CHART
items.desc: Guitar and ukulele chord reference. Browse chord voicings with fingering diagrams. Joystick selects chord and variant; button switches instrument. Data from tombatossals/chords-db.

items.name: CIRCLE OF 5THS
items.desc: The circle of fifths as an interactive wheel. Rotate to any key to see its name, sharps and flats, relative minor, and the chords that live in it — the map every musician keeps in their head.

controls.Left/Right: Rotate selection around the circle (by fifths)
controls.Up/Down: Cycle view: key names / key signatures / chord progressions

items.name: DRUM MACHINE
items.desc: A Roland TR-808-style step sequencer. 16 steps, multiple tracks for kick, snare, hi-hat, and more. Beat patterns scroll and animate.

items.name: GRAMOPHONE
items.desc: A spinning disc, a tonearm, and a brass horn. The platter turns, the arm traces the groove.

items.name: HURDY GURDY
items.desc: Medieval string instrument where a rosined wheel acts as a continuous bow. Keys press tangents against the strings. Drone notes sustain while the melody plays above.

items.name: LATIN DNA
items.desc: The rhythmic DNA of Latin music. Clave patterns — son, rumba, bossa nova — shown as timeline grids with accent markers. The two-bar key that unlocks an entire tradition.

items.name: LATIN GROOVES
items.desc: Latin dance rhythms animated as layered percussion parts. Salsa, samba, bossa nova, cumbia, and more — each groove broken into its component instruments.

items.name: METRONOME
items.desc: A mechanical metronome with swinging pendulum and sliding weight. Sets the tempo for practice and performance.

items.name: MUSIC BOX
items.desc: A rotating cylinder with pins plucking a metal comb. Each pin triggers a note as the barrel turns. The original mechanical music player.

items.name: PIANO ROLL
items.desc: A scrolling piano roll showing notes in time. Colored bars descend onto a keyboard. The format dates back to player pianos of the 1900s and is still used in MIDI editors.

controls.Up/Down: Cycle song pattern (Classical, Ragtime, Jazz, Pop)

items.name: RUDIMENTS
items.desc: All 40 international drum rudiments. Animated sticking patterns with R/L hand indicators and accent marks. The fundamental vocabulary of percussion.

items.name: SCALES
items.desc: A scale reference over a piano keyboard. Cycle through scales and modes, transpose to any root, and toggle between note names, interval numbers, and scale degrees.

controls.Left/Right: Transpose root note (C, C#, D, ... B)
controls.Action: Toggle note names / interval numbers / scale degrees

items.name: SYNTHESIZER
items.desc: A modular synthesizer with oscillators, filters, and patch cables. Waveforms morph on screen as parameters change.

items.name: THEREMIN
items.desc: The only instrument you play without touching. Side view with pitch antenna, volume loop, and electromagnetic field arcs. Waveform display shows the output.

items.name: TURNTABLE
items.desc: A vinyl record spinning with a tonearm tracking the groove. The platter rotates at 33 RPM, the stylus rides the spiral.

items.name: AUDUBON
items.desc: Plates from John James Audubon's Birds of America, panning slowly across the panel. Life-size hand-colored engravings of American birds.

items.name: CLOUDS
items.desc: Procedurally generated clouds drifting across the sky. Perlin noise fields produce layered formations that shift and dissolve.

items.name: DRIFT 3D
items.desc: Comanche-style voxel space terrain flyover. Height maps and color maps rendered with ray marching to produce rolling landscapes, water, and atmospheric fog.

items.name: EROSION
items.desc: Hydraulic erosion simulation. Water droplets carve channels through procedural terrain, depositing sediment in valleys and deltas.

items.name: FIREFLIES
items.desc: Kuramoto model of coupled oscillators. Each firefly has its own internal clock but synchronizes with visible neighbors — the same behavior seen in real firefly swarms.

items.name: GOULD
items.desc: Plates from John Gould's ornithological folios — hummingbirds, toucans, and the birds of Australia — auto-panning across the grid.

items.name: HAECKEL
items.desc: Ernst Haeckel's Art Forms of Nature: radiolarians, jellyfish, and diatoms drawn with symmetric precision. The plates that fused biology and Art Nouveau.

items.name: LAKE
items.desc: A lake surface with ripples, reflections, and gentle wave motion. Pixels shimmer as if catching light on water.

items.name: MERIAN
items.desc: Maria Sibylla Merian's metamorphosis studies — caterpillars, butterflies, and their host plants — from her groundbreaking Surinam fieldwork.

items.name: REDOUTE
items.desc: Pierre-Joseph Redouté's botanical watercolors — the roses and lilies painted for Empress Joséphine's garden at Malmaison.

items.name: RIPPLES
items.desc: Concentric rings expand from random drop points, interact, and fade. A rain-on-water simulation using the 2D wave equation with damping. Interference patterns form where rings collide.

items.name: WATER CYCLE
items.desc: The hydrological cycle animated: evaporation, cloud formation, precipitation, runoff, and groundwater flow. A continuous loop of water moving through the Earth system.

items.name: WEATHER
items.desc: Animated weather scenes: rain, snow, thunderstorms, sunshine. Particles fall with realistic physics, lightning flashes illuminate the grid.

items.name: ATLAS
items.desc: A browsable world atlas on the LED grid. Pan across terrain, satellite imagery, night lights, political borders, elevation, and historical empire overlays, all baked from real map data.

items.name: CLOVERLEAF
items.desc: A highway interchange with looping ramps. Cars flow through the cloverleaf pattern, merging and diverging without stopping.

items.name: GREENWAVE
items.desc: Traffic signals timed so that a car traveling at the speed limit hits green lights all the way down a corridor.

items.name: GRIDLOCK
items.desc: The BML traffic model. Cars move east or north on alternating steps. Below a critical density, traffic flows. Above it, the system jams completely. A phase transition you can see.

items.name: HIGHWAY
items.desc: Multi-lane highway traffic with lane changes, speed differences, and phantom traffic jams. Congestion waves propagate backward through the flow — the accordion effect made visible.

items.name: ROAD
items.desc: A single road with cars following each other. Car-following models produce stop-and-go waves from nothing — no accident, no bottleneck, just the physics of human reaction times.

items.name: ROTARY
items.desc: A traffic roundabout with entering and circulating vehicles. Yield-on-entry dynamics create a self-regulating flow. The European alternative to the four-way stop.

items.name: SIGNALS
items.desc: A signalized intersection with timed red/green phases. Cars queue, the light changes, the queue discharges.

items.name: SIGNS
items.desc: Street signs from around the world — stop signs, speed limits, yield signs, and highway markers from 6 continents. 63 signs across 6 categories.

items.name: BRAIN
items.desc: A spiking neural network with excitatory and inhibitory neurons. Regular spiking, chattering, and bursting patterns. Membrane potential dynamics with real spike generation.

items.name: CELL
items.desc: A data-driven cross-section of a living cell. 14 real biochemical pathways: ATP synthase spinning, ribosomes translating mRNA, protein complexes cycling through conformational states.

items.name: DNA
items.desc: The central dogma of molecular biology, animated. Six scenarios: double helix structure, replication, transcription, codon reading, mutation, and chromatin packing.

items.name: ELECTRONS
items.desc: What electrons actually do. A tour of physics experiments, energy transitions, chemical reactions, quantum phenomena, and semiconductor devices — each a looping scene with electrons and photons in motion.

items.name: ELEMENTS
items.desc: Animated Bohr models for all 118 elements. Electrons orbit on shells, nuclei pulse. Element categories color-coded: alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, lanthanides.

items.name: LATTICES
items.desc: 3D rotating unit cells of real crystalline structures: diamond, salt, ice, metals. Tiled cells with edge fading show infinite periodicity.

items.name: MATTER
items.desc: The three phases of matter — solid, liquid, and gas — simulated with interacting particles. Temperature controls phase transitions: melting, boiling, condensation, and freezing made visible.

items.name: Maxwell's Demon
items.desc: A thought experiment made visible. A demon sorts fast and slow gas particles between two chambers, seemingly violating the second law of thermodynamics.

controls.Left/Right: Slow down / speed up the simulation

items.name: MICROSCOPE
items.desc: Scenes viewed through a virtual microscope: microorganisms, cell structures, and crystal formations.

items.name: MOLECULES
items.desc: 3D ball-and-stick models with CPK coloring (carbon black, oxygen red, nitrogen blue). Real molecules rotating in space: amino acids, sugars, neurotransmitters.

controls.Up/Down: Cycle through groups (ALL, BASICS, DNA, etc.)

items.name: ORBITALS
items.desc: The shapes electrons take around atoms. Hydrogen-like s, p, d, and f orbitals, sigma and pi bonds, sp/sp²/sp³ hybrids, and the delocalized clouds of benzene and metals — rendered from real wavefunctions.

items.name: PEPTIDES
items.desc: Amino acids and peptides as rotating 3D bead-on-chain models. Each residue is a shaded sphere linked by backbone bonds, with disulfide bridges and ring closures, alongside a calculator-style info panel.

items.name: PROTEINS
items.desc: Real protein backbone structures from the RCSB Protein Data Bank. Cα coordinates with color-coded secondary structure: helices in red, sheets in yellow, coils in gray.

controls.Joystick: Rotate protein in 3D (pauses auto-cycle to let you explore)
controls.Z or X: Cycle through proteins in current category (resumes auto-cycle)
controls.Z+X: Cycle through category playlists (NOBEL, ANCIENT, ANIMALS, etc.)

items.name: RADIOACTIVE
items.desc: A grid of atoms spontaneously decays, emitting alpha, beta, and gamma particles. Five scenarios demonstrate different decay modes. Each event is probabilistic — quantum randomness made visible.

items.name: SPECTROSCOPE
items.desc: Real element emission spectra at their correct wavelengths. Each element has a unique fingerprint of spectral lines — this is how we know what distant stars are made of.

controls.Up/Down: Cycle element groups (ALL, NOBLE, ALKALI, etc.)

items.name: CHLADNI
items.desc: Sand on a vibrating plate collects at the nodes, revealing standing wave patterns. Each frequency produces a unique geometric figure.

items.name: COLOR MIX
items.desc: Computational fluid dynamics with colored dyes mixing in a flow field. Pigments swirl, fold, and blend according to Navier-Stokes equations.

items.name: COLOR THEORY
items.desc: Color wheel, complementary colors, and color mixing. Additive and subtractive models side by side on the LED grid.

items.name: CONVECTION
items.desc: Rayleigh-Bénard convection cells. Heated fluid rises, cool fluid sinks, forming rolling convection cells with characteristic aspect ratios.

items.name: COULOMB
items.desc: Coulomb electrostatics with field-line tracing and equipotential rendering. Five scenarios — dipole, quadrupole, motor, circuit, and random — all governed by the inverse-square law.

controls.Single button: Toggle view mode (field lines / equipotential / circuit wire)

items.name: FLUID PLAY
items.desc: Stir a real fluid with the joystick. Jos Stam's Stable Fluids solver runs live — drag the cursor and colored density swirls, folds, and diffuses behind it.

items.name: INK DROPS
items.desc: Drops of colored ink falling into a fluid simulation. The ink spreads, curls, and diffuses according to Navier-Stokes equations.

items.name: OPTICS
items.desc: Geometric ray tracing from first principles. Light enters a prism and splits into spectral colors at their correct refractive indices. Other scenarios show lenses, mirrors, and kaleidoscopes.

items.name: OSCILLOSCOPE
items.desc: Phosphor-green vintage oscilloscope with sine, square, triangle, and sawtooth waveforms. Lissajous mode draws figures from frequency ratios — the same patterns used to calibrate TV broadcasts.

items.name: WAVE TANK
items.desc: The 2D wave equation solved with leapfrog integration. Point sources, double-slit diffraction, two-source interference, ripples, and reflection. Real wave physics on a grid.

items.name: WIND TUNNEL
items.desc: Computational fluid dynamics with obstacles. Semi-Lagrangian advection shows vortex shedding, turbulence, and flow separation around objects. Real aerodynamics at 64×64.

items.name: WIND TUNNEL LAB
items.desc: Move a solid obstacle through a flowing wind tunnel and watch the wake respond — vortices shed and reattach as you reshape the flow. Powered by Stam's Stable Fluids solver.

items.name: CONSTELLATIONS
items.desc: Real star positions from the Hipparcos catalog (ESA, 1997) with constellation lines from Stellarium. Stars rendered at correct relative brightness. Joystick to rotate the sky.

items.name: EARTH
items.desc: A rotating globe showing continents, oceans, and the terminator (day/night boundary). The planet as seen from space, rendered on a 64×64 pixel sphere.

items.name: ECOSYSTEM
items.desc: A food web with an energy budget. Five trophic levels — soil, grass, herbivore, predator, apex — each eat, move, reproduce, and die. The 10% rule emerges on its own: always far fewer apex predators than herbivores.

items.name: EVOLUTION
items.desc: Natural selection in action. Organisms compete for resources, reproduce with mutation, and adapt over generations. Fitness landscapes shift as populations explore genetic space.

items.name: GENETIC DRIFT
items.desc: Allele frequencies shift through random sampling in finite populations. Watch genetic diversity rise and fall without selection pressure — pure chance shaping evolution.

items.name: MOON PHASES
items.desc: The lunar cycle animated with astronomically correct illumination. The terminator sweeps across the Moon’s surface from new to full and back.

items.name: MOVEMENT
items.desc: The mechanics of how a body moves. A stick figure built on Vitruvian proportions cycles through walk, run, and sneak gaits, driven by procedural biomechanical models — 14 joints and 15 bones in motion.

items.name: PREDATOR-PREY
items.desc: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey dynamics. Two populations locked in oscillation — more prey means more predators, more predators means fewer prey, and the cycle repeats.

items.name: SEISMIC
items.desc: Seismic wave propagation through layered earth. P-waves compress, S-waves shear, surface waves roll. Wavefronts refract at layer boundaries following Snell’s law.

controls.Action: Trigger quake (at cursor, or cycle positions if neutral)

items.name: SOLAR SYSTEM
items.desc: Newtonian gravity with Velocity Verlet integration. Planets orbit at correct relative speeds around a central star.

items.name: TECTONIC
items.desc: Tectonic plates shifting, colliding, and diverging. Subduction zones, rift valleys, and mountain building driven by convection currents in the mantle.

items.name: 3 BODY PROBLEM
items.desc: Three bodies orbiting under mutual gravitational attraction. No closed-form solution exists — the system is inherently chaotic. Watch them dance, fling each other away, and sometimes find temporary stability.

items.name: ART GALLERY
items.desc: A slideshow of the famous paintings, cycling through five centuries of art history.

items.name: CHILL
items.desc: Relaxing, ambient visuals that drift slowly — the calm end of the collection.

items.name: COMPLEXITY
items.desc: Complex systems and emergent behavior — automata, flocks, reaction-diffusion, and self-organizing patterns.

items.name: CUSTOMS
items.desc: Your own exported GIF animations, played back as a slideshow. Make them in PAINT GIF.

items.name: DEMOSCENE
items.desc: Old-school demo effects back to back — plasma, twisters, rotozoom, and the rest of the demoscene canon.

items.name: EDUCATION
items.desc: School-friendly educational visuals gathered for classroom use — science, math, and culture.

items.name: ENERGY
items.desc: High-energy, intense visuals — strobing color, fast motion, maximum activity.

items.name: MUSIC MIX
items.desc: Every music visual in one stream — instruments, sequencers, and rhythm charts.

items.name: NATURALIST
items.desc: The scientific-illustration plates shuffled together — Audubon, Haeckel, Merian, and more.

items.name: SCIENCE LAB
items.desc: The physics and biology simulations, auto-cycling — fluids, fields, orbits, and cells.

items.name: TITLE
items.desc: A parade of the Wonder Cabinet title screens — 70+ boot cards and marquees.

items.name: ABOUT
items.desc: Hardware and build details for the cabinet — what's inside and who made it. A second page hides a retro terminal HELLO WORLD easter egg.

items.name: BRIGHTNESS
items.desc: Adjust the LED panel's overall brightness, with a live preview. Turn it down for a dim room or up to fill a bright one.

items.name: CONTROLS
items.desc: A live map of the panel's controls. Press any button or push the joystick and watch it light up — the quickest way to check the inputs are working and learn the layout.

items.name: CREDITS
items.desc: A scrolling roll of every source, inspiration, and attribution behind the cabinet's games and visuals.

items.name: EFFECTS
items.desc: Choose which transition effects the idle screensaver uses when it cross-fades between visuals. Toggle any you don't like off.

items.name: GAMMA
items.desc: Fine-tune the panel's gamma curve and toe-lift so dark tones read correctly, with a live before/after preview. A calibration control, not a toy.

items.name: IDLE MIX
items.desc: Curate what the idle screensaver shows. Set how heavily each category is weighted, and mark individual visuals as favorites or blacklist them entirely.

controls.Button: Cycle state: normal -> favorite -> blacklisted -> normal

items.name: IDLE TIMERS
items.desc: Set the cabinet's timing: how long before the idle screensaver kicks in, how fast idle visuals cycle, and when the panel sleeps.

items.name: INFO
items.desc: Live Raspberry Pi vitals — CPU temperature, IP address, uptime, and memory use. Handy for setup and troubleshooting.

items.name: INTERNET
items.desc: Join a WiFi network using only the joystick and buttons. Scan for networks, enter the password on a scroll-wheel keyboard, and connect — no keyboard needed.

items.name: PAINT
items.desc: A digital painting canvas. Move the cursor to paint with light. Brush color and size cycle through palettes.

items.name: PAINT GIF
items.desc: A frame-by-frame animation studio. Draw 64×64 pixel art with per-frame undo, onion-skinning, and live preview, then export an animated GIF. Your creations show up in the CUSTOMS mix.

items.name: PIXEL TEST
items.desc: A diagnostic that exercises every one of the 4,096 LEDs across all primary colors — a marching cursor, quadrant tests, and full-field fills — to catch dead or miswired pixels.

items.name: SAFETY
items.desc: Accessibility settings in one place: reduce flashing for photosensitivity, switch on colorblind-friendly palettes, and other display-comfort options.

items.name: SHUTDOWN
items.desc: Safely powers down the Raspberry Pi so you can unplug the cabinet without corrupting its storage. Always shut down here before pulling the plug.

items.name: STATS
items.desc: A scrolling record of play history and high scores across the cabinet's games.

controls.Left/Right: Switch pages (ALL, Overview, Most Played, High Scores, Leaderboard)
controls.Up/Down: Scroll content (speed control on ALL page, directional on sections)

items.name: UPDATE
items.desc: Update the cabinet to the latest software over the internet and restart, or roll back to the version it ran before the last update if something goes wrong.
